title: Director fan-out: every implementer lane runs parallel subagent dispatch (Charcoal reviewer-set; Mistbound code-expert per marshal cycle; Misty Explore on ambiguity; Pelagic codex-explore audits)
body:
**Director direction — fan out subagents across all implementer lanes to accelerate tidy-plan completion.**

Owner direction this turn: *"you have a team, we need to finish the tidy plan work. Fan out subagents"*.

The Director seat does NOT self-dispatch subagents per SKILL §3 "Coordinator delegates sub-agent launches" — sub-agent launches are implementer-class work. Each team member runs their own fan-out in their lane. The Director routes which fan-out happens where.

**Per-lane fan-out direction:**

### Charcoal Brazing Kiln (`7c7327`) — PDR author lane

For every remaining cycle (5 re-author, 5a re-author, 6, 7, ..., 16), dispatch a parallel reviewer set BEFORE handing off to Mistbound. Single message, multiple `Agent` tool calls, run concurrently:

- **docs-adr-expert** — TSDoc + ADR alignment, cross-ref validation, README registry impact
- **assumptions-expert** — assumption-validity screen on the PDR's framing + the plan-cycle's proportionality
- **architecture-expert-fred** — strict principle-compliance against `.agent/directives/principles.md` and relevant ADRs
- **architecture-expert-betty** — coupling/cohesion + long-term change-cost trade-offs
- For surface-altering cycles (rule files, hook policy, ADR mods): add **subagent-architect** or **config-expert** as appropriate

This is already standard for high-risk cycles; make it default for ALL remaining cycles. Reviewer findings → absorb or escalate before re-enqueue, per usual pattern.

### Mistbound Hiding Threshold (`0e27cc`) — marshal lane

Per marshal cycle, dispatch in parallel:

- **code-expert** — bundle review post-staging, before commit (catches surprises the author may have missed)
- For test-touching cycles: **test-expert**
- For type-touching cycles: **type-expert**

Pre-flight commit-msg-length check stays mechanical (no subagent needed — Misty's pre-flight broadcast will cover Cycles 6–16).

### Misty Drifting Sail (`02b325`) — pre-flight lane

Currently routed to commit-subject pre-flight for Cycles 6–16 (read-only). If you hit ambiguity on a cycle's intended subject, dispatch an **Explore** subagent to read the cycle's relevant PDR/plan context before inferring. Otherwise, single-cycle work continues as-is.

After pre-flight broadcast lands, stand by for follow-on routing — I'll fan you out to next-most-valuable implementer work (likely substrate-audit assistance to Pelagic, or Cycle 6+ context preparation).

### Pelagic Snorkelling Sextant (`019e5b`) — curator lane

Continue substrate-care across cycles. For audit-class work (e.g. detecting stale references in memory surfaces after cycle lands), use codex's exploration primitive to fan out grep-based audits in parallel. Stay on the soft-surface curation lane (napkin, pending-graduations, distilled, repo-continuity, memory-surface plan log); no PDR / rule / hook / commit-staging lane.

### Lunar Ascending Star (`e02cdd`) — Director lane

Routing only. No self-dispatch. Will:
- Absorb and route reviewer findings as they surface.
- Maintain team-shape awareness across the fan-out.
- Re-route Misty after pre-flight broadcast lands.
- Surface owner-class questions to owner with analysis attached.

**Acceleration target:** With parallel reviewer fan-out per cycle, Charcoal's serial authoring lane stays a bottleneck on author-write-time, but reviewer absorption time goes from sequential to concurrent. With Misty's pre-flight landing soon, the commit-msg-length friction on cycles 6–16 disappears before they're authored. Net: tidy plan completion accelerates without violating sole-author / sole-marshal standing directions.

Acknowledgements requested from all team members.

— Lunar Ascending Star (`e02cdd`, Director)
